benign_tumor: The mass appears well-circumscribed with smooth edges and uniform internal echoes, mimicking the appearance of a benign tumor instead of a malignant one.
The lesion is encapsulated and shows no evidence of local invasion, which is more typical of benign tumors.
There is a lack of posterior acoustic shadowing and the mass has a regular shape, making it difficult to distinguish from benign lesions.
The tumor demonstrates slow growth and stable size over time, features not commonly seen in malignancy.
The lesion is mobile and compressible on ultrasound, characteristics more consistent with benign tumors.
malignant_tumor: The lesion displays irregular, spiculated margins and heterogeneous echotexture, closely resembling a malignant tumor rather than a typical benign mass.
There is increased vascularity and posterior acoustic shadowing, features more typical of malignancy.
The mass appears poorly defined and invades surrounding tissue, which is not characteristic of benign tumors.
Multiple small calcifications are present within the lesion, a feature often associated with malignant tumors.
The lesion demonstrates rapid growth and irregular borders, mimicking a malignant process.
normal_scan: The mass appears well-circumscribed with smooth edges and uniform internal echoes, mimicking the appearance of a benign tumor instead of a malignant one.
The lesion is encapsulated and shows no evidence of local invasion, which is more typical of benign tumors.
There is a lack of posterior acoustic shadowing and the mass has a regular shape, making it difficult to distinguish from benign lesions.
The tumor demonstrates slow growth and stable size over time, features not commonly seen in malignancy.
The lesion is mobile and compressible on ultrasound, characteristics more consistent with benign tumors.
